content:
- Hosted run #39 on commit 4852f61 completed with the intended workflow behavior: validate-fast and validate-long both passed, all publish-image jobs passed, quay-security-gate waited for validation, surfaced policy_failure with explicit findings, workflow-summary reported that raw Quay publication had succeeded, and publish-manifest was skipped honestly.
- Created follow-on bug .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-verify-image-arm64-has-quay-vulnerability-findings.md because the remaining blocker is a real verify arm64 image vulnerability set, not a workflow-control bug.
- Next after this context window, there is no remaining work on story-26 task 01 itself; the task is complete and ready for the final Ralph/task-finished commit that includes the bookkeeping files and new bug record.

- Started Choose-Task turn.
- Read prior progress log entry: previous run finished story-26 task 01 workflow behavior and created a follow-on bug about arm64 Quay vulnerability findings.
- Next: inspect current_tasks/current_tasks_done, resolve blockers per instructions, and write the selected task path to .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Refreshed the Ralph task summary and opened the two currently active task files.
- Inspecting blocker tags directly because selection depends on real task-file state, not the truncated summary excerpt.
- Also checking the repository manifests/workflows to ground the decision in the actual stack and architecture.

- Refreshed current task summaries and confirmed there are exactly two active candidates.
- Repository stack check: Rust workspace (`crates/runner`, `ingest-contract`, `operator-log`, `setup-sql`), Go verify service under `cockroachdb_molt/molt`, and GitHub Actions workflow `publish-images.yml`.
- Direct tag scan on both active task files found no `<blocked_by>` tags; the bug task is unblocked, failing, and `high` priority, so it outranks the suspended non-bug task.

- Selected the next task and wrote it to `.ralph/current_task.txt`.
- Chosen task: `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-verify-image-arm64-has-quay-vulnerability-findings.md`.
- Why: `.ralph/current_tasks.md` has two active failing tasks; direct file inspection found no `<blocked_by>` tags on either, and the bug task is unblocked with `<priority>high</priority>`, so BUG-FIRST precedence makes it mandatory before the suspended non-bug task.
- Actual repo stack/architecture confirmed while selecting: Rust workspace (`crates/runner`, `ingest-contract`, `operator-log`, `setup-sql`), Go verify service under `cockroachdb_molt/molt`, and GitHub Actions workflow `publish-images.yml`.
- Next after quitting immediately due to context limit: execute the selected bug task with red-green TDD, then validate `make check`, `make test`, `make lint`, and `make test-long` if impacted.

- Read previous Ralph progress, `.ralph/current_task.txt`, and the required `improve-code-boundaries` and `tdd` skills.
- Confirmed the selected task is `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-verify-image-arm64-has-quay-vulnerability-findings.md`; next I am reading the task file to determine whether this turn is plan-only (`TO BE VERIFIED`) or execution (`NOW EXECUTE`).
- Should do next if quitting immediately: inspect the task file status marker, then either create/update the required plan under the task plans subdir or start red-green execution exactly from the approved plan.

- Determined the task is in the planning branch because the bug task file had no plan marker or `<plan>` path yet.
- Inspected the verify image Dockerfile, current `cockroachdb_molt/molt/go.mod`, the Rust verify-image/source contract helpers, and OSV advisory records for the reported GHSA IDs.
- Confirmed the findings map to modules currently below fixed versions in this repo: `github.com/jackc/pgx/v5` (`5.5.1` < `5.9.0`), `google.golang.org/grpc` (`1.61.0` < `1.79.3`), and `golang.org/x/crypto` (`0.18.0` < `0.31.0` and `< 0.35.0`). Next is to write the plan file, add its path to the task, flip the plan terminator to `NOW EXECUTE`, and quit immediately.

- Wrote the planning artifact `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-verify-image-arm64-has-quay-vulnerability-findings_plans/2026-04-20-verify-image-vulnerability-remediation-plan.md` using the required `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` workflow.
- Added the `<plan>` path to `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-verify-image-arm64-has-quay-vulnerability-findings.md` and, after validating the interface/boundary choices, flipped the plan terminator from `TO BE VERIFIED` to `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next after quitting immediately due to context limit: read the task and plan again, then execute the plan in vertical red-green slices starting with a failing Rust contract for the vulnerable verify-module dependency floor.

- Read prior Ralph progress, `.ralph/current_task.txt`, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skills.
- Confirmed the active task is `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-verify-image-arm64-has-quay-vulnerability-findings.md`, and prior progress says planning is complete with the plan already flipped to `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next: read the task file and referenced plan, then execute it in vertical red-green slices and keep appending progress as I go.

- Added the first repo-owned verify-source contract test and deepened the existing `VerifySourceContract` helper to parse retained `go.mod` requirements and compare semver floors.
- Ran `cargo test -p runner --test verify_source_contract verify_source_keeps_pgx_at_or_above_the_security_floor`; it failed red as intended because `github.com/jackc/pgx/v5` is still `v5.5.1` and below the required `v5.9.0` floor.
- Next: raise only the `pgx` floor in `cockroachdb_molt/molt`, refresh the module graph, then rerun the focused verify-source contract and any immediately exposed focused Go checks.

- Completed the first two red-green slices: added failing Rust verify-source contract tests for `pgx >= v5.9.0` and `grpc >= v1.79.3`, then used `GOTOOLCHAIN=auto go get ...` to lift those module floors until each focused contract went green.
- The `grpc` floor update also transitively lifted `golang.org/x/crypto` from `v0.18.0` to `v0.46.0`, so the original crypto finding no longer held at the repo-owned module boundary; I folded that verification into the verify-source contract coverage and wired the rest of the existing verify-source assertions into a real top-level test file to satisfy `cargo clippy -D warnings`.
- Next: run manual version verification plus the required repo gates (`make check`, `make lint`, `make test`), then update the task bookkeeping only if every required lane passes.

- Began execution and completed two red-green slices: added top-level Rust verify-source contracts, raised `github.com/jackc/pgx/v5` to `v5.9.0`, and raised `google.golang.org/grpc` to `v1.79.3` in `cockroachdb_molt/molt`.
- While manually re-verifying the remaining `golang.org/x/crypto` finding, I discovered the approved design was wrong for indirects: `go.mod` no longer listed `x/crypto`, but `GOTOOLCHAIN=auto go list -m all` still resolved `golang.org/x/crypto v0.0.0-20210921155107-089bfa567519`, so the current contract source can produce a false green.
- Per task instructions, I updated the plan file `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-verify-image-arm64-has-quay-vulnerability-findings_plans/2026-04-20-verify-image-vulnerability-remediation-plan.md` back to `TO BE VERIFIED` with the discovered design flaw and quit immediately. Next turn should redesign the indirect/runtime dependency verification boundary before continuing execution.

- Re-read the bug task and plan; the task is still in the planning branch because the plan ends with TO BE VERIFIED.
- Inspected the current verify-source Rust helper, the new top-level Rust contract file, and the updated Go manifest. Confirmed the current helper only reads go.mod text, which is honest for direct requirements but not for resolved indirect runtime modules.
- Next: inspect the verify build-target dependency graph/module metadata to redesign the plan around a real build boundary for indirect module floors, then update the plan to NOW EXECUTE and quit immediately.

- Re-verified the design and updated the plan file `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-verify-image-arm64-has-quay-vulnerability-findings_plans/2026-04-20-verify-image-vulnerability-remediation-plan.md`.
- Corrected the boundary: security-floor assertions must use the Docker-built verify image artifact via `VerifyImageArtifactHarness` and `go version -m` on the extracted `/usr/local/bin/molt`, not raw `go.mod` text or `go list -m all`.
- Manual planning verification on the current working tree: the built image artifact embeds `github.com/jackc/pgx/v5 v5.9.0`, embeds `google.golang.org/grpc v1.79.3`, and does not embed `golang.org/x/crypto`.
- Flipped the plan terminator from `TO BE VERIFIED` to `NOW EXECUTE` and stopped immediately per task instructions.
- Next turn should read the task and plan again, then execute the revised plan in vertical red-green slices starting with a failing `verify_image_contract` test for the embedded `pgx` floor.

- Resumed the active bug task by reloading previous progress, the current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skills.
- Confirmed prior work ended with the approved plan flipped to NOW EXECUTE after redesigning the dependency verification boundary around the built verify image artifact.
- Next: read the task file and the referenced plan again, then execute the plan in strict red-green slices starting from the verify image contract that inspects the built artifact.

- Completed TDD slice 1 at the correct boundary. Added a new verify-image contract for `github.com/jackc/pgx/v5 >= v5.9.0` and first ran it red as a compile failure because the image artifact harness lacked embedded-module inspection.
- Made that slice green by deepening `VerifyImageArtifactHarness`: it now extracts `/usr/local/bin/molt` from the built Docker image, runs `go version -m` against the extracted binary, parses embedded `dep` lines, and asserts semver floors inside the harness.
- Next: add the next RED contract for `google.golang.org/grpc >= v1.79.3`, run it focused, then keep the boundary cleanup moving by removing the misplaced source-side security-floor checks.

- Confirmed the remaining red was the wrong boundary: `cargo test -p runner --test verify_source_contract verify_source_keeps_grpc_at_or_above_the_security_floor` failed because `golang.org/x/crypto` is absent from `go.mod`, which is acceptable at the shipped-binary boundary.
- Moved the remaining security policy fully into `verify_image_contract`: added image-level `grpc` and `x/crypto` tests, added `assert_embedded_module_is_absent_or_meets_minimum_version` to `VerifyImageArtifactHarness`, and removed the misplaced source-side security floor assertions/helpers.
- Caught and fixed a cleanup regression where `verify_source_stays_scoped_to_the_verify_only_slice` lost its `#[test]` marker; focused reruns are now green for source scope plus the new `grpc` and `x/crypto` image policies.
- Next: run the full focused verify contract files, then move into the verify Go slice tests/build and finally the required repo-wide gates (`make check`, `make lint`, `make test`).
